focus_keyword: Crispy Parmesan Chicken with Garlic Cream Sauce
topic: Crispy Parmesan Chicken with Garlic Cream Sauce
recipe_text:
Title: Crispy Parmesan Chicken with Garlic Cream Sauce
Description: Experience the perfect blend of crunchy, cheesy chicken paired with a creamy garlic sauce that’s savory and luxurious. It’s comfort food at its finest!
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 35 min
Total: 1 hr
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 590 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1 cup Panko breadcrumbs
- 1 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 1 teaspoon dried Italian seasoning
- 4 tablespoons olive oil
- 4 tablespoons unsalted butter
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
- 1 cup chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley, for garnish

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
3. In a shallow dish, place flour and season with salt and pepper. In another dish, whisk eggs and water together. In a third dish, mix Panko breadcrumbs, Parmesan cheese, and Italian seasoning.
4. Dredge each chicken breast in flour, shaking off excess, then dip in the egg mixture, and finally coat in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing lightly to adhere.
5. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breasts and cook until golden brown on each side, about 3-4 minutes per side. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
6. In a separate saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Add garlic and cook for about 1 minute, until fragrant.
7. Stir in flour and cook for another minute. Gradually whisk in chicken broth and cream. Cook, whisking continuously, until the sauce is smooth and thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
8. Remove sauce from heat and stir in Parmesan cheese until melted and smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Remove chicken from the oven. Serve the chicken topped with the garlic cream sauce and garnish with chopped parsley.

topic: Garlic-Parmesan Cottage Cheese Bagels – Sefez Recipes
recipe_text:
Title: Garlic-Parmesan Cottage Cheese Bagels – Sefez Recipes
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 25 min
Total: 35 min
Servings/Yield: 6 servings
Calories: 220 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- Quick to prepare and perfect for breakfast or snacks.
- Ingredients
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 cup cottage cheese
- 2 large eggs
- 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- Extra Parmesan cheese for topping
- Prep Time:
- 10 minutes
- Cooking Time:
- 25 minutes
- Total Time:
- 35 minutes
- Servings:
- 6 bagels
- Calories:
- ~220 kcal per bagel

Instructions:
1. Step 1: Preheat oven
2. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
3. Step 2: Mix dry ingredients
4. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
5. Step 3: Mix wet ingredients
6. In another bowl, mix cottage cheese, eggs, olive oil, garlic, and Parmesan until smooth.
7. Step 4: Form dough
8. Gradually stir wet ingredients into dry until a dough forms.
9. Step 5: Knead dough
10. Knead on a floured surface for 2–3 minutes until smooth.
11. Step 6: Shape bagels
12. Divide dough into 6 portions and shape each into a ball.
13. Step 7: Create bagel holes
14. Poke a hole in each ball and stretch to form bagels.
15. Step 8: Prepare for baking
16. Place bagels on the prepared baking sheet and sprinkle extra Parmesan on top.
17. Step 9: Bake
18. Bake for 20–25 minutes until golden and cooked through.
19. Step 10: Cool and serve
20. Allow bagels to cool slightly before serving.
21. Frequently Asked Questions
22. Can I use Greek yogurt instead of cottage cheese?
23. Yes, Greek yogurt works, but it may make the dough slightly softer.
24. Can I freeze these bagels?
25. Yes, allow them to cool completely, then freeze in an airtight container for up to 1 month.
26. Are these bagels chewy like traditional bagels?
27. They’re softer and more tender than traditional boiled bagels, with a slightly denser texture.
28. Final Thoughts
29. These Garlic-Parmesan Cottage Cheese Bagels are soft, cheesy, and full of flavor. Easy to make and versatile, they’re perfect for breakfast, brunch, or a savory snack any time of day.

topic: Keto Chicken Bacon Ranch Taquitos: Easy Low-Carb Recipe! – Simply Yummy Eats
recipe_text:
Title: Keto Chicken Bacon Ranch Taquitos: Easy Low-Carb Recipe! – Simply Yummy Eats
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 30 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 300 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- Start by melting mozzarella cheese in the microwave for about 30-60 seconds.
- Once melted, use a spatula to spread the cheese on parchment paper, shaping it into a circle.
- Let it cool slightly until it’s manageable, then peel it off carefully.

Instructions:
1. 1. Prepping Your Oven:
2. First things first, let’s preheat your oven. Set it to 400°F (200°C). While that heats up, line a baking sheet with parchment paper to make cleanup a breeze later.
3. 2. Mixing the Filling:
4. Grab a large mixing bowl and add in the shredded chicken, softened cream cheese, ranch dressing, chopped bacon, green onions, cheddar cheese, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder (if you’re using it), and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix everything together really well until it’s all combined. This is where the magic happens!
5. 3. Making the Cheese Tortillas:
6. If you’re using cheese tortillas, melt the mozzarella cheese in either a microwave or on the stovetop until it’s nice and gooey. Once melted, use parchment paper and a spatula to shape the melted cheese into tortilla shapes. This part may take a little practice, but it’s fun!
7. 4. Filling the Tortillas:
8. Now that your tortillas are ready, take a spoonful of that tasty chicken mixture and place it in the center of each tortilla. Roll it up tightly, making sure to tuck in the ends to keep all that goodness inside. Isn’t it looking great?
9. 5. Baking the Taquitos:
10. Arrange your rolled taquitos seam-side down on the baking sheet. Repeat this process with the rest of your mixture and tortillas. Pop the baking sheet into the oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes. Keep an eye on them until they’re golden brown and crispy!
11. 6. Serving Your Taquitos:
12. Once they’re out of the oven, let the taquitos cool for a few minutes. They’re best served warm, and feel free to dip them in some extra ranch dressing for an added flavor boost!
13. Enjoy your delicious and low-carb Keto Chicken Bacon Ranch Taquitos!
14. Can I Use Store-Bought Shredded Chicken?
15. Absolutely! Store-bought shredded chicken is a great time-saver. Just make sure it’s fully cooked and preferably from a rotisserie chicken for added flavor. You can season it further with salt and pepper before mixing with the other ingredients.
16. Can I Use Regular Tortillas Instead of Cheese Tortillas?
17. If you’re not concerned about keeping it keto, you can use regular flour or corn tortillas. However, keep in mind that this will increase the carb count. If you want to stick to keto, try to find low-carb or high-fiber tortillas available at most grocery stores.
18. How Should I Store Leftover Taquitos?
19. Store any leftover taquitos in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days. To reheat, place them in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 10-15 minutes until heated through and crispy again. You can also use an air fryer for extra crispiness!
20. Can I Make These Taquitos in Advance?
21. Yes! You can prepare the taquitos, roll them, and store them unbaked in the fridge for up to a day. If you want to freeze them, place them on a baking sheet to freeze individually before transferring them to a freezer-safe bag. Bake from frozen for about 20-25 minutes when you’re ready to enjoy them!

topic: Garlic Butter Steak Bites: A 15-Minute Miracle Dinner
recipe_text:
Title: Garlic Butter Steak Bites: A 15-Minute Miracle Dinner
Description: Quick and flavorful garlic butter steak bites with zucchini, perfect for a low-carb weeknight dinner. This recipe is ready in just 15 minutes, making it ideal for busy home cooks.
Prep: 5 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 15 min
Servings/Yield: 2 servings
Calories: 350 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 1 lb steak (sirloin, ribeye, or New York strip), cut into 1-inch cubes
- 1 medium zucchini, cut into half-moons
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
- Optional: Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish

Instructions:
1. Pat the steak bites dry with paper towels. Season them with salt and pepper.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add steak bites to the hot skillet in a single layer. Sear for 1-2 minutes per side until browned. Remove steak from skillet and set aside.
4. Add butter to the same skillet. Once melted, add minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Add the zucchini to the skillet. Cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender-crisp.
6. Return the steak bites to the skillet. Toss everything together to coat with the garlic butter.
7. Cook for another 1-2 minutes until the steak is heated through.
8. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired. Serve immediately.

focus_keyword: How to Make Grilled Chicken u0026amp; Broccoli Bowls with Creamy Garlic Sauce
topic: How to Make Grilled Chicken u0026amp; Broccoli Bowls with Creamy Garlic Sauce
recipe_text:
Title: How to Make Grilled Chicken u0026amp; Broccoli Bowls with Creamy Garlic Sauce
Description: Juicy grilled chicken, tender steamed broccoli, and hearty rice come together in a wholesome bowl finished with a creamy garlic sauce. Balanced, flavorful, and ready in just 30 minutes.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 30 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 310 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- For the bowls:
- 2 medium boneless, skinless chicken breasts
- 4 cups fresh broccoli florets
- 2 cups cooked brown rice (or quinoa)
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- Pinch of paprika (optional)
- For the creamy garlic sauce:
- ½ cup plain nonfat Greek yogurt
- 2 tbsp light mayonnaise
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1–2 tbsp water (to thin)
- Salt and pepper, to taste

Instructions:
1. Grill the ChickenPreheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, and paprika. Grill for about 6–7 minutes per side, or until fully cooked (internal temp 165°F/74°C). Let rest, then slice.
2. Steam the BroccoliWhile chicken cooks, steam broccoli florets for 4–5 minutes, until bright green and just tender.
3. Make the SauceIn a small bowl, whisk together Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, minced garlic, lemon juice, water, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy. Adjust thickness with a little more water if desired.
4. Assemble the BowlsDivide cooked rice evenly among 4 bowls. Top each with sliced grilled chicken and steamed broccoli. Drizzle with creamy garlic sauce just before serving.

recipe_text:
Title: BBQ Chicken Coleslaw Wraps
Description: There’s something timeless about gathering around a table with smoky, savory barbecue chicken as the centerpiece. But in the heat of summer—or on those busy weeknights when the oven feels like too much—BBQ Chicken Coleslaw Wraps bring all the flavor without the fuss. They’re fresh, tangy, hearty, and deeply satisfying, marrying two comforting classics: barbecue chicken and creamy coleslaw, all wrapped up in a soft tortilla.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 20 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 380 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 2 cups cooked shredded chicken (rotisserie or grilled)
- 1/2 cup BBQ sauce (your favorite style)
- 2 cups coleslaw mix (shredded cabbage and carrots)
- 1/4 cup mayonnaise
- 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar
- 1 teaspoon honey
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 4 large flour tortillas or wraps
- Optional: chopped fresh parsley or cilantro for garnish

Instructions:
1. Step 1: Prepare the ChickenIn a medium bowl, combine the shredded chicken with BBQ sauce. Stir until every piece is well coated. If the chicken is cold, you can warm it slightly in the microwave or a pan over low heat for that fresh-from-the-grill flavor.
2. Step 2: Make the ColeslawIn a separate bowl, mix the mayonnaise, apple cider vinegar, honey, salt, and pepper. Whisk until smooth. Add the coleslaw mix and toss until evenly coated. The vinegar and honey give the slaw a light, balanced flavor that pairs perfectly with the bold BBQ chicken.
3. Step 3: Assemble the WrapsLay the tortillas flat on a clean surface. Spoon a generous amount of BBQ chicken down the center of each wrap, followed by a scoop of creamy coleslaw. Sprinkle with fresh parsley or cilantro if using.
4. Step 4: Roll and ServeFold in the sides, then roll up tightly from the bottom. Slice in half if desired. Serve immediately for best texture, or wrap in parchment for an easy grab-and-go lunch.

topic: Honey Lime Chicken & Avocado Rice Stack for Easy Meal Prep
recipe_text:
Title: Honey Lime Chicken & Avocado Rice Stack for Easy Meal Prep
Description: This Honey Lime Chicken & Avocado Rice Stack is a vibrant and nutritious meal that's perfect for quick meal prep.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 30 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 450 calories
Cuisine: Healthy
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- 4 pieces Chicken Breasts (Use thighs for juicier option)
- 1/4 cup Honey (Cook until bubbly for best flavor)
- 2 tablespoons Lime Juice (Fresh juice is optimal)
- to taste Seasonings (salt, pepper, etc.) (Adjust according to taste)
- 1 cup Rice (Brown rice for extra fiber or quinoa for lower-carb option)
- 2 pieces Avocado (Select ripe but firm avocados for slicing)

Instructions:
1. Pound the chicken breasts to an even thickness and season with salt and pepper.
2. Mix honey and lime juice to create the glaze. Cook chicken in a skillet over medium-high heat for approximately 6-7 minutes per side, glazing during the last minute.
3. Cook the rice according to package instructions. Fluff with a fork and season with salt.
4. Slice the avocado just before assembling to keep it fresh.
5. Layer the rice, glazed chicken, and sliced avocado in a ring mold. Drizzle with more lime juice before serving.

focus_keyword: Chipotle Chicken Bowl with Black Beans, Corn, & Creamy Sauce
topic: Chipotle Chicken Bowl with Black Beans, Corn, & Creamy Sauce
recipe_text:
Title: Chipotle Chicken Bowl with Black Beans, Corn, & Creamy Sauce
Description: A smoky and flavorful chicken bowl layered with black beans, corn, avocado, and drizzled with creamy chipotle sauce. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or meal prep.
Prep: 30 min
Cook: 15 min
Total: 45 min
Servings/Yield: 2 servings
Calories: 520 calories
Cuisine: Mexican-Inspired
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 2 tablespoons lime juice
- 2 cloves garlic minced
- 1 teaspoon chipotle chili powder
- 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
- 1 teaspoon cumin
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
- 1 cup cooked black beans
- 1 cup corn grilled, roasted, or canned
- 1 avocado sliced
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro chopped
- 1/2 cup sour cream or Greek yogurt
- 1 tablespoon chipotle peppers in adobo minced
- 1 teaspoon garlic powder
- 1 teaspoon honey
- 2 tablespoons lime juice (for sauce)

Instructions:
1. Marinate the chicken with olive oil, lime juice, garlic, chipotle chili powder, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper for at least 30 minutes.
2. Cook the chicken on a grill or skillet until fully cooked and lightly charred. Let rest, then slice.
3. Warm black beans and season with cumin and salt. Prepare corn (grilled, roasted, or canned).
4. Make the creamy sauce by whisking sour cream, lime juice, minced chipotle peppers, garlic powder, and honey.
5. Assemble the bowl with beans, corn, avocado slices, chicken, sauce, and garnish with cilantro.

focus_keyword: High Protein Garlic Parmesan Pasta Bowls (384 Cal + 36g Protein)
topic: High Protein Garlic Parmesan Pasta Bowls (384 Cal + 36g Protein)
recipe_text:
Title: High Protein Garlic Parmesan Pasta Bowls (384 Cal + 36g Protein)
Description: This creamy garlic parmesan pasta bowl is the perfect balance of flavor and fuel—high in protein, low in fuss. With tender grilled chicken, a rich but lightened-up parmesan sauce, and perfectly cooked pasta, it’s a meal prep hero that keeps you full and satisfied. Say goodbye to boring lunches.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 15 min
Total: 25 min
Servings/Yield: 1 servings
Calories: 384 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 120 g cooked penne or rigatoni
- 120 g grilled chicken breast, sliced
- 1 tsp olive oil
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 30 g light cream cheese
- 15 g grated parmesan
- 30 ml unsweetened almond milk (or milk of choice)
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- Fresh parsley or spinach (optional)

Instructions:
1. Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and set aside.
2. In a pan, heat olive oil and sauté garlic until fragrant.
3. Add cream cheese and almond milk, stirring until smooth. Mix in parmesan and season with salt and pepper.
4. Stir in the pasta and toss until coated in the sauce.
5. Top with grilled chicken and optional greens like spinach or parsley.
6. Divide into meal prep containers and refrigerate for up to 4 days.

focus_keyword: Spicy Garlic Chicken and Broccoli Noodle Bowls Delight You!
topic: Spicy Garlic Chicken and Broccoli Noodle Bowls Delight You!
recipe_text:
Title: Spicy Garlic Chicken and Broccoli Noodle Bowls Delight You!
Description: A delicious and flavorful dish featuring tender chicken, fresh broccoli, and noodles tossed in a spicy garlic sauce.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 35 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 450 calories
Cuisine: Asian
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 1 lb chicken breast, sliced
- 2 cups broccoli florets
- 8 oz noodles (your choice)
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chili paste
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:
1. Cook the noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
3. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant.
4. Add sliced chicken and cook until browned and cooked through.
5. Add broccoli florets and stir-fry for about 3-4 minutes.
6. Stir in soy sauce and chili paste, mixing well.
7. Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and toss everything together.
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Serve hot and enjoy!

topic: Chicken and Garlic Gravy with Cheesy Mashed Potatoes
recipe_text:
Title: Chicken and Garlic Gravy with Cheesy Mashed Potatoes
Description: Golden seared chicken breasts served over creamy, cheesy mashed potatoes and drenched in rich garlic gravy. This comforting, flavorful dinner is a go-to for cozy nights and special weekends alike.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 30 min
Total: 40 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 520 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs
- 6 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
- 1 ½ cups chicken broth
- ¼ cup heavy cream (optional)
- 2 pounds Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cubed
- ½ cup milk
- 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- 1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley or chives, for garnish

Instructions:
1. Season chicken with salt and pepper. In a skillet over medium-high heat, sear chicken in oil until golden and cooked through (internal temp 165°F). Set aside and keep warm.
2. Reduce heat to medium. In the same skillet, melt butter and sauté minced garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
3. p id=”instruction-step-3″>3. Stir in flour to create a roux. Cook 1-2 minutes while stirring.
4. p id=”instruction-step-4″>4. Gradually whisk in chicken broth, breaking up lumps. Simmer until thickened. Stir in heavy cream if using. Add salt and pepper to taste.
5. p id=”instruction-step-5″>5. Boil potatoes in salted water for 12–15 minutes until tender. Drain.
6. id=”instruction-step-6″>6. Mash potatoes with butter, milk, and cheddar cheese. Season with salt and pepper.
7. id=”instruction-step-7″>7. Plate mashed potatoes, top with sliced chicken, and pour garlic gravy over. Garnish with herbs and serve.

Total: 1 hr
Servings/Yield: 8 servings
Calories: 300 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

- 8 whole wheat tortillas
- 1 cup Greek yogurt
- 1/2 cup shredded cheese
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper

topic: Sweet Potato Taco Bowl: Colorful Comfort Food Made Easy
recipe_text:
Title: Sweet Potato Taco Bowl: Colorful Comfort Food Made Easy
Description: This Sweet Potato Taco Bowl is a vibrant and customizable dish perfect for quick dinners.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 30 min
Total: 45 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 550 calories
Cuisine: Mexican
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- 4 cups Sweet Potatoes (Cubed)
- 2 tablespoons Olive Oil (Or avocado oil)
- 1 teaspoon Smoked Paprika (Or regular paprika)
- to taste Salt
- to taste Pepper
- 1 pound Ground Beef (Or turkey, chicken, or black beans)
- 2 tablespoons Taco Seasoning (Homemade or store-bought)
- 1 cup Pico de Gallo (Diced tomatoes, onion, cilantro)
- 1 cup Guacamole (Mashed avocado with lime juice)
- 1/2 cup Sour Cream (Or Greek yogurt)
- to taste Optional Toppings (Crumbled cheese, lime wedges, tortilla chips)

Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss cubed sweet potatoes with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway through.
2. While sweet potatoes are roasting, heat a skillet over medium heat. Add ground beef, breaking it up as it cooks for 6-8 minutes. Stir in taco seasoning and a splash of water, simmer for 2-3 minutes.
3. Prepare pico de gallo by mixing diced tomatoes, onion, cilantro, jalapeño, and lime juice in a bowl. Let sit for a few minutes.
4. Assemble the bowl by layering roasted sweet potatoes, taco beef, pico de gallo, guacamole, and sour cream in individual serving bowls.
5. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lime wedges. Add crumbled cheese or tortilla chips if desired, and serve immediately.

recipe_text:
Title: Cheesy Chicken Broccoli Orzo
Description: A quick and creamy one-pot pasta dish made with orzo, tender chicken, fresh broccoli, and melty cheddar cheese—perfect for busy weeknights.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 30 min
Servings/Yield: 6 servings
Calories: 550 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Dish

Ingredients:
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tbsp butter
- ½ medium onion (chopped)
- 3 cloves garlic (minced)
- 2 cups broccoli florets (fresh or thawed frozen)
- ½ tsp Italian seasoning
- 1 cup orzo pasta
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
- 2 cups cooked (shredded chicken (e.g., rotisserie))
- 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese (preferably sharp)
- Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions:
1. Heat oil and butter in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté onions for 3–4 minutes.
2. Add broccoli; cook 2 more minutes.
3. Stir in garlic, seasoning, and orzo; toast for 30 seconds.
4. Pour in broth, cream, and Worcestershire sauce. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer.
5. Cook uncovered for 10 minutes, stirring often.
6. Add chicken, stir to heat through.
7. Remove from heat and stir in cheddar cheese until melted.
8. Season with salt and pepper. Let sit 2–3 minutes to thicken.

topic: Keto Philly Cheesesteak Rolls: 5 fast, easy, delish bites.
recipe_text:
Title: Keto Philly Cheesesteak Rolls: 5 fast, easy, delish bites.
Description: Keto Philly Cheesesteak Rolls: A quick, keto-friendly handheld bite that captures the flavors of the classic sandwich with beef, peppers, onions, and melted cheese wrapped in a low-carb option.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 15 min
Total: 25 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 300 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- 1 lb thinly sliced beef (ribeye or sirloin works best)
- 1 tbsp olive oil or butter
- 1 small onion, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 1 cup shredded mozzarella or provolone cheese
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce (optional)
- Low-carb wraps or mozzarella cheese slices (for rolling)

Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onions and peppers, and sauté until soft and golden, about 5–6 minutes. Remove and set aside.
2. Add sliced beef to the same skillet and cook until browned. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and Worcestershire sauce if using.
3. Mix the cooked vegetables back into the beef, then sprinkle cheese on top. Let it melt slightly and stir to combine.
4. Lay out low-carb wraps or large cheese slices. Spoon a portion of the cheesesteak mixture onto each one and roll tightly.
5. Optional: bake at 375°F (190°C) for 5–7 minutes to crisp up the edges or melt cheese further.
6. Serve warm, with a side of sugar-free dipping sauce if desired.

recipe_text:
Title: Keto Pepperoni Cheese Crisps - skilledcooks
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 35 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 140 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- Pepperoni slices – Choose regular or turkey pepperoni for a leaner option.
- Cheddar cheese – Shredded or sliced. You can also use mozzarella , parmesan , or pepper jack for variety.

Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat.
3. Place cheese mounds (about 1 tablespoon each) spaced evenly apart. If using sliced cheese, cut into squares or rounds.
4. Top each mound with one pepperoni slice .
5. Bake for 6–8 minutes , or until the cheese is bubbly and edges turn golden brown.
6. Remove from oven and allow to cool on the tray for 5 minutes—this helps them crisp up properly.

topic: High-Protein Chicken Ranch Quesadilla : Crispy, Creamy & Low-Carb - The recipe mingle
recipe_text:
Title: High-Protein Chicken Ranch Quesadilla : Crispy, Creamy & Low-Carb - The recipe mingle
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 20 min
Total: 35 min
Servings/Yield: 10 servings
Calories: 300 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Main Course

Ingredients:
- Gathering Your Flavor Crew
- This is where the fun begins! The beauty of this recipe is in its simple, powerhouse ingredients. Each one plays a specific role in creating that perfect, crave-worthy bite. Here’s what you’ll need:
- 1 Low-Carb Tortilla (like Mission Carb Balance):
- This is our foundation! These tortillas are fantastic because they’re high in fiber, keeping the net carbs low while giving us that pliable, crispy vessel we need.
- Chef’s Insight:
- Don’t have a low-carb one? Any whole wheat or flour tortilla will work beautifully—just be mindful of the macros.
- 1/2 cup Shredded Cooked Chicken:
- The protein star! I love using rotisserie chicken for ease and flavor, but any leftover baked or grilled chicken works.
- Pro-Tip:
- Shred it yourself for the best texture—it holds the creamy filling so much better than cubed chicken.
- 1 tablespoon Plain Greek Yogurt:
- Our secret weapon for creaminess and a protein boost! It replaces heavy, calorie-laden mayo or sour cream, giving us that tangy kick without the guilt.
- Substitution:
- If you’re out of Greek yogurt, light sour cream is a great stand-in.
- 1 Laughing Cow Cheese Wedge (Light or Original):
- This little wedge is a game-changer! It melts into an incredibly smooth, velvety sauce that binds everything together.
- Chef’s Insight:
- It’s a low-fat, high-flavor miracle worker. If you must substitute, a tablespoon of cream cheese will do the trick.
- 1/4 cup Shredded Mozzarella Cheese:
- Hello, gorgeous cheese pull! Mozzarella is our melty, stretchy hero.
- Pro-Tip:
- I always recommend shredding your own cheese from a block. Pre-shredded cheese is coated with anti-caking agents that can make it less melty.
- 1/2 teaspoon Ranch Seasoning:
- The flavor bomb! This little bit of seasoning powder packs a huge punch of herby, tangy goodness.
- Substitution:
- You can make your own by mixing dried dill, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder, and a pinch of salt.
- 1/2 tablespoon Butter:
- For that perfect, golden-brown, crispy exterior.
- Chef’s Insight:
- Butter gives us that irresistible flavor and color. For a dairy-free option, a light spray of avocado or olive oil works well, but butter is king for crispiness!
- Let’s Build Some Quesadilla Magic!
- Alright, team, let’s fire up the stove! This comes together in a flash, so have all your ingredients ready to go. We’re going for maximum efficiency and flavor.
- Make the Filling:
- In a medium bowl, combine your shredded chicken, Greek yogurt, Laughing Cow cheese wedge, shredded mozzarella, and ranch seasoning. Now, get in there with a fork and mix it all up until it’s beautifully combined and creamy.
- Chef’s Hack:
- If your Laughing Cow cheese is a little cold and stubborn, microwave the whole bowl for 10 seconds to soften it up—it’ll mix like a dream! The goal is a uniform, deliciously coated chicken mixture.
- Assemble with Care:
- Lay your tortilla flat on a cutting board. Spoon the creamy chicken mixture onto one half of the tortilla, spreading it out evenly but leaving a small border around the edge. This prevents the filling from oozing out during cooking. Now, gently fold the bare half of the tortilla over the filling, creating a perfect half-moon. Press down lightly to seal the edges.
- Cook to Golden Perfection:
- Place a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add your butter and let it melt, swirling it around to coat the pan. Once it’s sizzling gently, carefully place your quesadilla in the center of the pan.
- Chef’s Pro-Tip:
- Resist the urge to crank the heat! Medium heat is our friend here. It allows the inside to get hot and melty while the outside turns a gorgeous, crisp golden brown without burning.
- The Flip:
- Cook for 2-3 minutes on the first side. You’ll know it’s ready to flip when the bottom is golden brown and the edges look crispy. Now, for the moment of truth! Slide your spatula under the filled side, and with a confident (but gentle!) wrist motion, flip that beauty over. Cook for another 2-3 minutes on the second side until it matches the first in golden glory.
- The Rest (The Hardest Part!):
- Once it’s cooked, transfer your masterpiece to a cutting board. This is crucial! Let it rest for just one minute before slicing.
- Why?
- This allows the molten cheese filling to set slightly, so when you cut into it, it doesn’t immediately gush out everywhere. It’s a tiny step that makes a huge difference in presentation.
- Plating Up the Goodness
- How you serve this can take it from great to absolutely legendary! I love slicing it into three triangles—it just makes it more fun to eat. Now, for the accompaniments:
- For Dipping:
- A side of cool, creamy Greek yogurt mixed with a little lime juice and cilantro is my healthy go-to. If you’re feeling a bit spicy, a vibrant salsa or pico de gallo adds a fresh, acidic kick that cuts through the richness perfectly. And for those days when you just need a little extra, a light drizzle of sriracha or a dollop of guacamole is pure heaven.
- For a Full Meal:
- Turn this into a complete, restaurant-worthy plate! Serve it alongside a crisp green salad with a zesty vinaigrette, or a cup of black bean soup. The contrast of the hot, crispy quesadilla with a cool, refreshing salad is a textural dream come true.
- Make It Your Own!
- One of the best things about cooking is making a recipe sing your song. This quesadilla is a fantastic canvas for your creativity. Here are a few of my favorite twists:
- The “Everything but the Kitchen Sink”:
- Add in some finely diced bell peppers (any color!), a tablespoon of sweet corn, and a few slices of pickled jalapeños for a sweet, crunchy, and spicy kick.
- The Buffalo Lover’s Dream:
- Swap the ranch seasoning for a tablespoon of Buffalo sauce mixed right into the chicken filling. Use a pepper jack cheese instead of mozzarella for an extra kick, and serve with a side of blue cheese or ranch dressing for dipping. *Chef’s kiss!*
- The Veggie-Packed Powerhouse:
- Sneak in a handful of fresh spinach (wilt it and squeeze out the water first) or some sautéed mushrooms. It’s an easy way to get more greens in without compromising flavor.
- The Turkey Club Twist:
- Swap the chicken for shredded turkey and add a tablespoon of finely chopped cooked bacon. It tastes just like a classic club sandwich, but in delicious, handheld form!
- The Taco Tuesday Vibe:
- Replace the ranch seasoning with your favorite taco seasoning. It’s a simple swap that completely transforms the flavor profile!
- From My Kitchen to Yours
- This recipe has been a constant in my life for years, and it’s evolved in the best ways. I remember once, in a hurry during a crazy catering prep, I accidentally used a garlic and herb Laughing Cow wedge. It was a happy accident that was so good, I now recommend it as a regular variation! It’s these little kitchen experiments that often lead to the best discoveries.
- The core of this dish, though, remains the same: it’s about nourishment, speed, and satisfaction. It’s a testament to my grandmother’s teaching that good food doesn’t have to be fussy. It just has to be made with intention and a whole lot of love—even if that love only takes 10 minutes to show itself.
- Your Questions, Answered!
- I’ve gotten so many DMs about this recipe, so let’s tackle the most common questions right here.
- Q: My quesadilla is burning on the outside before the cheese melts inside. What am I doing wrong?
- A: This is almost always a heat issue! You’re cooking on too high of a heat. Lower it to a solid medium, and be patient. The gentle heat will penetrate the tortilla and warm the filling perfectly, giving you that crispy-yet-melty result we’re after.
- Q: Can I make a big batch of the filling ahead of time?
- A: Absolutely! The filling will keep beautifully in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days. This makes throwing together a quick lunch or snack even faster. Just give it a stir before assembling your quesadilla.
- Q: My quesadilla falls apart when I flip it! Help!
- A> No worries! This is a common fear. First, make sure you’re not overstuffing it—a solid, even layer is better than a massive pile. Second, use a good, wide spatula to get fully under the filled side for support. And third, a little confidence goes a long way! A quick, decisive flip is better than a slow, hesitant one.
- Q: Can I make this in an air fryer?
- A: You sure can! Spray the outside of the assembled quesadilla with a little oil and air fry at 370°F (190°C) for about 5-6 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden and crisp. It works like a charm!
- Feeding Your Body & Soul
- I always say food should make you feel good, both during the meal and after. This High-Protein Chicken Ranch Quesadilla is designed to do exactly that. It’s a macro-balanced powerhouse that’s perfect for a post-workout refuel, a satisfying lunch, or a light dinner.
- Estimated Nutrition Per Serving (1 full quesadilla):
- Calories:
- ~350
- Protein:
- ~40g (This is the star! It’s incredibly satiating.)
- Carbohydrates:
- ~22g (Net Carbs)
- Fat:
- ~15g
- Remember, these are estimates and can vary based on the specific brands of ingredients you use. The high protein and fiber content from the chicken and low-carb tortilla help keep you full and energized for hours, making it a truly guilt-free pleasure.
- Keep Cooking with Passion!
- And there you have it, my friends—my beloved High-Protein Chicken Ranch Quesadilla. It’s simple, it’s speedy, and it’s packed with the kind of flavor that makes you look forward to eating well. This recipe is a little piece of my kitchen philosophy: be resourceful, be bold, and always, always cook with joy.
- I hope this recipe brings as much easy deliciousness to your kitchen as it has to mine. Don’t be afraid to play with it and make it your own. That’s where the real magic happens. If you make it, I’d be thrilled if you tagged me on social
- @ChefHannahWhitecare
- so I can see your beautiful creations!
- Until next time, keep your knives sharp and your passion for food even sharper.
- With love and a pinch of sass,
- Hannah

focus_keyword: Low Carb Cheesy Garlic Chicken Wraps Recipes
topic: Low Carb Cheesy Garlic Chicken Wraps Recipes
recipe_text:
Title: Low Carb Cheesy Garlic Chicken Wraps Recipes
Description: These low carb cheesy garlic chicken wraps are loaded with tender chicken, oozy cheese, savory garlic, and wrapped in low-carb tortillas. A quick, satisfying, and keto-friendly option for lunch or dinner.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 15 min
Total: 30 min
Servings/Yield: 4 servings
Calories: 370 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dinner

Ingredients:
- 2 cups cooked chicken breast, shredded
- 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
- 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/4 cup cream cheese, softened
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 4 large low-carb tortillas or wraps
- 1 tablespoon fresh parsley, chopped
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:
1. Preheat a skillet or grill pan over medium heat and add olive oil.
2. In a bowl, mix together the shredded chicken, mozzarella cheese, cheddar cheese, cream cheese, minced garlic, parsley, salt, and pepper until well combined.
3. Divide the chicken and cheese mixture evenly among the low-carb tortillas. Roll each tortilla tightly to form wraps.
4. Place the wraps seam side down in the preheated skillet. Cook each wrap for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the wraps are golden brown and the cheese is melted.
5. Remove from skillet, slice in half, and serve hot. Garnish with extra parsley if desired.
